c.d: Number of active users on the platform. A larger user base increases the potential for sales.
The cost associated with listing and selling items on the app, including listing fees, commission fees, and transaction fees.
The variety of items that can be sold on the app (e.g., clothing, electronics, furniture, handmade items).
The available methods for shipping items to buyers, including seller-arranged shipping, prepaid labels, and integration with shipping services.
How simple and intuitive the app is to use for listing items, managing inventory, and communicating with buyers.
Whether the app primarily focuses on local sales or offers the ability to sell internationally.
The features and resources available to sellers, such as listing templates, analytics, and advertising options.
The extent to which the app fosters a sense of community among buyers and sellers through features like verified profiles and ratings systems.
The methods available for processing payments and how secure and efficient the payment process is.
The average rating of the app in app stores, reflecting user satisfaction and app quality.
